methods.method_name: Phylogenetic Analysis of Flagellum Coding Genes
methods.description: This method studies the evolutionary pattern of flagellum coding genes in diatoms and other algae using a maximum likelihood relationship approach.

steps.description: Sequences for the IFT172 and IFT88 genes involved in the flagellum development identified in L. danicus were downloaded from NCBI nucleotide database through homology searches using blastn.
steps.searchable_summary: download IFT172 and IFT88 gene sequences from NCBI using blastn

steps.description: Sequences were also obtained by local BLAST searches from the transcript dataset that constituted many publicly available transcriptomes of species sequenced within the Marine microbial eukaryotic transcriptome sequencing project (e-value smaller or equal to 1e-10).
steps.searchable_summary: perform local BLAST searches on transcript datasets with e-value <= 1e-10

steps.description: Obtained sequences were gathered into datasets and sequence alignment was generated using MUSCLE version 3.8.31.
steps.searchable_summary: align gene sequences using MUSCLE v3.8.31

steps.description: Phylogenies for each of the dataset was constructed following a maximum likelihood approach using the WAG amino acid substitution model with a discrete gamma distribution and nonparametric bootstrap of 1000 replicates.
steps.searchable_summary: construct phylogenies using maximum likelihood with WAG model and bootstrap
